chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-5
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Why coding style? Most time in SW lifecycle is spend on the maintenance phase This is mostly done by HUMANS  code readability helps Most time you maintain code of somebody else (and not easy to contact) Also: Ask yourself if you understand the code YOU wrote months ago… Important What is it? Programming style, also known as code style, is a set of rules or guidelines used when writing the source code for a computer program. It is often claimed that following a particular programming style will help programmers read and understand source code conforming to the style, and help to avoid introducing errors. Wikipedia

chunks.text: Elements of coding style Naming Conventions (variables, functions, classes) File Naming and Organization Code Formatting Code Comments Documentation Coding practices Best practices in how to code e.g. for security Important

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-9
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Benefits of good coding style Easier maintenance (big thing) Easier testing Easier optimization Easier portability Why coding style and benefits https://www.multidots.com/importance-of-code-quality-and-coding-standard-in-software-development/ Important

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-10
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Specific coding styles…. Often depend on languages Ton of good coding styles adopted and became de-facto standard Teams have to agree on coding style consistent with the company policy and best practices Standards change – follow them Enforce coding style by coding review (No coding style used  no submission!!!) CSC 648-848: Agree on a coding style after end of M2 Enforce it Will be checked and graded for Important

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-11
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Coding styles for Languages (examples) Java coding style http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/codeconventions-150003.pdf https://github.com/twitter/commons/blob/master/src/java/com/twitter/common/styleguide.md PHP coding style https://pear.php.net/manual/en/standards.php Python coding style https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/ https://google.github.io/styleguide/pyguide.html C++ coding style https://google.github.io/styleguide/cppguide.html HTML coding styles and checkers https://www.w3schools.com/html/html5_syntax.asp https://google.github.io/styleguide/htmlcssguide.html https://www.smashingmagazine.com/2008/11/12-principles-for-keeping-your-code-clean/

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-12

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-14
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Coding style for comments and documentation (examples) Comments can be for Headers (of classes, modules, functions) – basic information like purpose/function, methods, attributes, author, date In code Headers https://pear.php.net/manual/en/standards.header.php https://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/PHP_Programming/Coding_Standards#Header_Comment_Blocks General (Java, Python, C++) http://www.oracle.com/technetwork/java/javase/documentation/codeconventions-141999.html https://google.github.io/styleguide/pyguide.html#Comments https://legacy.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0008/#comments https://google.github.io/styleguide/cppguide.html#Comments

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-15
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Tools for automatic documentation generation (examples) Idea: Document code in a certain way Run tool to automatically generate documentation General overview https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Comparison_of_documentation_generators Doxygen – automatic doc. generation from source code About http://www.doxygen.nl/index.html How to document your code so doxygen can automatically create the documentation http://www.doxygen.nl/manual/docblocks.html Doxygen demo: https://youtu.be/44Ja2X_fzv4

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-16
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Doxygen - Documentation For Your Programs (Demo)

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-17
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Github documentation and comments Good comments are critical for being able to leverage github Think about looking for update on specific type of issue – search comments to find it quickly Research shows (including ours) that teams who do better github comments do better code and SW https://github.com/erlang/otp/wiki/writing-good-commit-messages Important

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-18
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Best Practices Coding Style for Security Ensure and enforce proper coding practices – use code and architecture reviews Check inputs and allow only valid ones Ensure proper transmission and management of passwords Use secure internet protocols Check buffer size – make sure no attack can be done by sending longer data streams Prevent injection of SQL code into the forms Check uploaded content for executables Virus check all uploaded content 20 Acknowledged use of SE book by Sommerville (2006), 8e Important

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-19
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Forms and Inputs – one of the KEY security issue on the WWW Cross site scripting, SQL and other injections – most done or first attempted via forms input Hence, follow best SE practices for validating and checking all form inputs  plenty of tools and coder and APIs available e.g. https://www.securecoding.cert.org/confluence/display/seccode/SEI+CERT+Coding+Standards go to java and check rule 00 https://wiki.sei.cmu.edu/confluence/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=88487865 https://1000hz.github.io/bootstrap-validator/ Acknowledged use of SE book by Sommerville (2006), 8e 21

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-20
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Coding in the AI era ~Henry’s POV From Implementation to AI-Augmented Engineering & Orchestration Working with AI coding assistants Architect AI-integrated systems Evaluate AI outputs critically Design for security in AI-driven environments Orchestrate hybrid human-AI workflows Shift: Engineer as Orchestrator From coder -> system thinker Implementer -> decision architect Syntax mastery -> problem framing mastery Human-in-the-loop engineering Code Review Secure Coding System Design Governance, Ethics & Professional Responsibility Engineering Productivity & Cognitive Offloading When AI enhances thinking When AI weakens thinking Maintaining core algorithmic skills Avoiding “AI dependency collapse”

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-21
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: Team Project Time Work on M2 More Detailed Requirements, Specs, Architecture, UI mock-ups and Vertical SW prototype. Two goals: More detailed design - Milestone 2 document: prioritize requirements; design high level UI mockups and storyboards; design high level DB organization, and practice UML. (one per team, submitted similarly like Milestone 1). Use as much space as you need, but the expected length is about 20-25 pages. You must submit all sections. Functional Requirements – prioritized UI Mockups and Storyboards (high level only) High level Architecture, Database Organization High Level UML Diagrams Identify actual key risks for your project at this time Project management Vertical SW Prototype: Develop first simple bare-bones prototype (from DB to browser) to test the infrastructure, educate the team, resolve technical issues and also serve as basis of further development (must run on the deployment server). (one per team) to be reviewed in class and also by Class CTO (off-line)

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-22
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: M2 MILESTONE 2. More Detailed Document. Make sure to read the instructions. Functional Requirements – prioritized Use M1 and reference numbers Prioritize (1 must have, 2 desired, 3 Opportunistic) UI Mockups and Storyboards (high level only). High level Architecture, Database Organization High Level UML Diagrams. Use UML to describe your class diagrams classes of core functionality. Identify actual key risks for your project at this time. Brainstorm with your team and list your identified risks (skills, schedule, technical, etc.) Project Management Assign tasks and describe how your team is working together.

chunks.chunk_id: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s)-23
chunks.source_file: SFSU CSC648-848 [02] Spring 2026 C7 Coding Style and Coding Practices (s).pptx
chunks.text: M2 – Vertical Prototype Vertical prototype [1] shall allow one to enter a search term on test home page (simple home page used to test vertical prototype), [2] then get a response form the DB and render it back on the browser in a simple test result page. UI for the test home page can be a simple one containing Class, section and team identification One free text entry field (this text is then used in SQL %LIKE search – check architecture) One pull down for 2-3 search parameter (search category like student or professional, major, roommate gender, room rent, etc.) – this is exercised using simple SQL filter – see architecture slides The test result page needs to display search results including images and later their thumbnails as well as profile videos (if implemented) in any reasonable layout (the goal here is to make sure you can access and display items and NOT the ultimate UI). Thumbnails need to be computed automatically using some open source thumbnail generation SW – you can do thumbnails later after you figure out the basic way to show imageThe DB can have only a few items. The items in the DB shall be encoded with full schema as it is defined by now in M2 document Make sure that if user hits “search” or related button, and does not enter any parameters, you display full list of items from the DB
